item.title: China Vanke, Country Garden Navigate Persistent Property Headwinds
item.description: Persistent weakness in China’s housing market will be the main drag when property developers China Vanke Co., Country Garden Holdings Co. and China Overseas Land & Investment Ltd. report next week.

item.title: Taiwan’s Largest ETF Set for Record Inflow, Defying War Angst
item.description: Taiwan’s largest equity exchange-traded fund is on track for record inflows this month, as domestic investors defy the global turmoil sparked by the Iran war and pile into the technology-heavy product.

item.title: Australia’s Fuel Supply Secure in Near Term, Albanese Says
item.description: Australia has a secure supply of fuel over the near term, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said Friday, as he sought to reassure skittish households and businesses at a time of mounting reports of gasoline shortages.

item.title: Malaysia Says Iran Has Allowed Some of Its Ships Through Hormuz
item.description: Iran has allowed Malaysian vessels trapped in the Persian Gulf to return home through the Strait of Hormuz, the Southeast Asian nation’s prime minister said in a televised address on Thursday evening.

item.title: TDR, I Squared Are Said to Select Banks for Aggreko’s US IPO
item.description: TDR Capital and I Squared Capital have picked banks to work on a US initial public offering for Aggreko Plc, one of the world’s biggest power generator rental firms, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Fund That Bet Big on Samsung Pushes for ADRs to Raise Valuation
item.description: A fund manager that has made Samsung Electronics Co. its biggest bet wants the South Korean company to list its shares in the US, a move it says would raise the chipmaker’s valuation.

item.title: Claude AI Maker Anthropic Said to Weigh IPO as Soon as October
item.description: Anthropic PBC is considering going public as soon as in October, according to people familiar with the matter, as the artificial intelligence company races with rival OpenAI Inc. to hold an initial public offering.

item.title: China Commerce Minister Protests US Probes Ahead of Trump Visit
item.description: Chinese Commerce Minister Wang Wentao raised “serious concerns” with US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer over new trade investigations, even as both sides signal a desire to stabilize relations before an expected presidential summit.

item.title: China’s Carmakers Return to Growth in Europe With EV Sales
item.description: Chinese automakers regained momentum in Europe in February, increasing pressure on Western incumbents after a brief sales slowdown at the start of the year.

item.title: MSCI Deadline Tests Indonesia’s Resolve to Fix Opaque Ownership
item.description: A looming MSCI Inc. decision over Indonesia’s market status is highlighting the challenge regulators face to address opaque ownership structures and poor disclosures by the nation’s biggest companies.

item.title: China Edges Toward Reflation, Boosting Stocks and Profit Outlook
item.description: China’s long-awaited shift toward reflation may finally be near, raising hopes it can revive corporate earnings and spur gains in stocks.

item.title: Dollar’s Best Month Since July Roils Wall Street’s FX Roadmap
item.description: The dollar is on track for its best month since July as the conflict in the Middle East scrambles Wall Street’s playbook for the world’s dominant reserve currency.

item.title: Kushner’s Thrive Holdings in Talks to Raise at Least $2 Billion
item.description: Thrive Holdings, an offshoot of Joshua Kushner’s venture capital firm Thrive Capital, is in discussions to raise at least $2 billion, according to people familiar with the talks.

item.title: BYD Stock Rebound Gathers Pace as Oil Shock Drives EV Sales Boom
item.description: BYD Co. shares are on track for their best month in over a year, as surging oil prices due to the Iran war brighten the outlook for electric vehicle sales.

item.title: Fed’s Barr Says It Makes Sense to Hold Rates Amid Uncertainty
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr said policymakers are well positioned to hold interest rates steady, as conflict in the Middle East and other factors complicate their ability to nudge inflation toward the 2% target.

item.title: Anthropic Wins Court Order Pausing Trump Ban on AI Tool
item.description: Anthropic PBC won a court order blocking a Trump administration ban on government use of the company’s artificial intelligence technology, after the Claude chatbot maker argued the move could cost it billions in lost revenue.

item.title: Investors Game Out Stagflation Risk in Japan as Oil Prices Rise
item.description: Some investors are starting to position for a scenario Japan hasn’t faced in decades, as rising oil prices and a weaker currency stoke stagflation worries and force a rethink of bets.

item.title: Gold Pares Losses as Trump Extends Deadline for Iran War Talks
item.description: Gold recovered some losses after US President Donald Trump again pushed back his deadline for securing a deal with Iran to end the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Oil Drops as Trump Pushes Back Timeline for Iran Energy Strikes
item.description: Oil fell after President Donald Trump again pushed back a deadline for striking Iran’s energy sites.

item.title: Antitrust Agencies Should Be Wary of AI Scrutiny, Official Says
item.description: The market for artificial intelligence is competitive, evolving rapidly, and antitrust enforcers should be wary of intervening, a senior White House official said.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Drop as Trump Extends Iran Talks: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian equities were set to fall after a plunge on Wall Street as traders assessed a 10-day extension to a deadline for Iran to strike a deal with the US or face more attacks.

item.title: Apple Discontinues Mac Pro Desktop in Favor of the Mac Studio
item.description: Apple Inc. on Thursday discontinued the Mac Pro desktop, its highest-end computer aimed at video editors, photographers and other advanced users.

item.title: Senate Bill Seeks to Curb Insider Bets on Prediction Markets
item.description: A bipartisan group of senators introduced legislation Thursday that would require lawmakers and government employees to disclose any bets they place through prediction markets in a bid to prevent public officials from profiting on privileged information.

item.title: Judge Defers Ruling on Multi-Color Chapter 11 Loan ‘Roll-Up’
item.description: A federal judge said he would let label-maker Multi-Color Corp. draw the rest of its $250 million Chapter 11 loan, but stopped short of approving a contested feature of the financing that would push some existing debt to the top of the repayment line.

item.title: Anatomy of a Deal: Inside the Epic Bidding War for Warner Bros.
item.description: On today’s Big Take podcast, why Netflix bowed out of the bidding for Warner Bros. Discovery, the regulatory hurdles that remain for Paramount and how these two big movie studios will become one.

item.title: Apple Gives iPhone Designers Rare Bonuses to Fight OpenAI Poaching
item.description: Apple Inc. awarded rare bonuses to iPhone hardware designers this week, aiming to stem a wave of departures to AI startups like OpenAI that are building their own devices.

item.title: Google Gemini Adds Tool to Make It Easier to Switch From ChatGPT
item.description: Google released new tools for its Gemini artificial intelligence assistant that will let users upload chat history and context from other AI apps, a move to lure users from rivals like OpenAI’s ChatGPT and Anthropic PBC’s Claude.

item.title: Ontario to Seed C$4 Billion Fund, Search for External Manager to Run It
item.description: The province of Ontario is seeding a new fund with as much as C$4 billion ($2.9 billion), aimed at encouraging pension funds and other investors to plow money into areas like artificial intelligence, defense and manufacturing.

item.title: OpenClaw Creator Says US Can Learn From China’s AI Adoption
item.description: While the AI tool has taken off in China, the reaction in the US has been more mixed

item.title: Fund With Stakes in Anthropic, SpaceX Slides After Short Report
item.description: Shares of the Fundrise Innovation Fund sharply reversed course Thursday, falling by a third as Citron Research revealed it was shorting the stock in the newly public venture capital investment vehicle.
